data.title: Lake Winn announces CFO transition
data.description:
• Lake Winn Resources (TSXV:LWR:CA) said that Binny Jassal resigned as the company’s CFO and as a director of the company.
  • Following Mr. Jassal’s resignation, James Ferreira has agreed to resume the role of interim CFO while the company searches for a suitable replacement.

data.title: Ukraine and U.S. ink memorandum of intent, paving way for critical minerals deal
data.description:
Ukraine has signed a memorandum of intent with the U.S., signaling progress towards a critical minerals deal that had been temporarily derailed following a heated meeting between presidents Donald Trump and Volodymyr Zelenskyy in February.

"We are happy to announce the signing, with our American partners, of a Memorandum of Intent, which paves the way for an Economic Partnership Agreement and the establishment of the Investment Fund for the Reconstruction of Ukraine," said Yulia Svyrydenko, Ukraine's economy minister.

"It is very important that the document notes the desire of the American people to invest together with the Ukrainian people in a free, sovereign, and secure Ukraine," she added.

Details of the pact were not disclosed. The text of the agreement needs to be finalized, signed, and then ratified by the countries' parliaments.

"There is a lot to do, but the current pace and significant progress give reason to expect that the document will be very beneficial for both countries," Svyrydenko noted. 

At a press event on Thursday alongside Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, Trump said a deal would be signed soon. "We have a minerals deal, which I guess is going to be signed next Thursday (April 24)? Soon. I assume they're (Ukraine) going to live up to the deal."

"We're still working on the details," Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent clarified, adding that the deal is substantially what was agreed upon previously. "We're shooting for around April 26."

data.title: Trump cues tit-for-tat China tariffs near end, TikTok deal may have to wait
XORTX Therapeutics receives Nasdaq notice for non-compliance
Japan sees relief in trade tensions, China snaps eight-day winning streak
data.description:
Most Asia-Pacific markets remained closed for Easter holiday, following the rise seen on the previous day. U.S. President Donald Trump's trade war remains a source of uncertainty. On Thursday, Trump offered some engaing signals that negotiations could lead to lower tariffs for some countries.

Japan's Nikkei (NKY:IND) rose 0.98% to 34,721, extending gains from the previous session amid hopes that ongoing trade talks between the US and Japan will lead to tariff concessions. Gains on previous day after Trump claimed 'big progress' in direct trade talks with a Japanese official. Early Friday, the U.S. dollar bought 132.42. The yield on Japan’s 10-year government bond remained steady around 1.3%.

On the data front, Japan's annual inflation rate edged to 3.6% in March 2025 from 3.7% in the prior month, marking the lowest print since last November. 

Taiwan's Taiex jumped 0.8%, and regional tech companies advanced, heavyweight Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM) reported a profit for the latest quarter, matching estimates

China's Shanghai Composite (SHCOMP) fell 0.42% to 3,266, snapping an eight-day winning streak as investors remained cautious amid ongoing uncertainty in Sino-US trade relations. China’s 10-year government bond yield held around 1.65%, as sentiment remained cautious amid ongoing uncertainty in Sino-US trade relations. The offshore yuan traded around 7.30 per dollar on Friday, as investors weighed ongoing uncertainty.

Late Thursday, President Trump signaled a potential easing of trade tensions with China, saying he does not want tariffs to increase further and may consider lowering them in the future.

Malaysia’s economy expanded 4.4% Y/Y in Q1. Malaysia's trade surplus increased to MYR 24.7 billion in March. Imports to Malaysia dropped 2.8% Y/Y to MYR 112.59 billion in March.

South Korea has proposed a supplementary KRW 12.2 trillion ($8.6 billion) budget to address mounting risks to growth from global trade tensions and domestic political uncertainty.

Hong Kong (HSI) closed.

India (SENSEX) closed.

Australia (AS51) closed.

Wall Street on Thursday ended mixed, driven by a post-earnings slump in industry bellwether UnitedHealth (UNH) and tensions between U.S. President Donald Trump and Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: Japan core inflation rate accelerates in March
data.description:
Japan's annual inflation rate edged to 3.6% in March 2025 from 3.7% in the prior month, marking the lowest print since last November. 

Food prices rose the least in three months even as rice costs jumped by 92.1% Y/Y due to poor harvests. Inflation also eased slightly for recreation, while education costs fell further.

Core consumer price index, which excludes fresh food but includes energy costs, rose 3.2% Y/Y in March 2025, up from 3% in February and in line with market expectations.

Monthly, the CPI increased 0.3%, after a 0.1% drop in February.

Japan-related ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWJ), (NYSEARCA:DXJ), (NYSEARCA:FXY), (NYSE:JEQ)

data.title: Independent bank corp sees loan growth and acquisition synergies in 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Independent Bank Corp. (INDB) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Jeffrey Tengel reported "solid pre-provision net revenue growth" for the quarter, driven by net interest margin improvement, strong fee revenue, and controlled expenses. He highlighted a 1.52% pre-provision net revenue return on average assets and an increase of 1.8% in tangible book value from the fourth quarter.
  • Tengel addressed elevated credit costs, noting these were linked to resolving previously identified problematic loans. He confirmed that resolutions for the largest non-performing loans (NPLs) are on track for the second quarter.
  • He emphasized the continued reduction in commercial real estate (CRE) concentration, with C&I and small business loans increasing by 2.1% and 2.6%, respectively, while CRE and construction loans decreased by 1.2% due to amortization and intentional reductions.
  • Tengel announced the addition of seven C&I bankers and two experienced hires to strengthen middle-market C&I and international banking efforts.
  • The pending acquisition of Enterprise Bancorp is expected to close in Q3 2025, with 32 out of 33 Enterprise commercial bankers retained. This acquisition will add approximately $1.5 billion in assets under administration (AUA) and support cross-sell opportunities.
  • CFO Mark Ruggiero noted GAAP net income for Q1 2025 at $44.4 million, with adjusted net income of $45.3 million after excluding merger-related expenses. Tangible book value per share increased by $0.85.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed guidance for low single-digit loan growth and low to mid-single-digit deposit growth for 2025. Non-interest income and expenses are also expected to grow at mid-single-digit rates.
  • Tengel confirmed plans for a core processing platform upgrade scheduled for May 2026, aiming to enhance operational efficiency and scalability.
  • Ruggiero highlighted that the subordinated debt raise in March would reduce net interest margin by approximately 11 basis points in Q2 but expects continued margin expansion of 3 to 4 basis points per quarter thereafter.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 adjusted diluted EPS was $1.06, and the return on average tangible common equity reached 9.01%.
  • Net interest margin improved to 3.42% on an FTE basis, supported by cash flow repricing dynamics and hedge maturities.
  • Deposit costs averaged 1.56%, with a 2.4% increase in period-end deposit balances compared to Q4 2024.
  • Significant charge-offs were made on large non-performing loans, including a $24.9 million charge-off for an office loan, which was slightly below the previously established reserve.

Q&A

  • Mark Fitzgibbon, Piper Sandler: Inquired about the resolutions of top NPLs and the confidence in resolving them. CEO Tengel explained that significant progress has been made, particularly for the largest loans, which are nearing resolution.
  • Steve Moss, Raymond James: Asked about the impact of the robust loan pipeline on growth expectations. Tengel noted that while the pipeline is healthy, ongoing reductions in CRE concentrations will temper overall growth.
  • Laurie Hunsicker, Seaport Research: Queried about the guidance impact of the subordinated debt raise. CFO Ruggiero explained that the debt's immediate margin impact would be mitigated over time by margin expansion from repricing activities.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concern over elevated credit costs and the resolution of large NPLs but acknowledged management's proactive measures and robust loan pipeline.
  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone, emphasizing progress in resolving credit issues and the expected benefits of the Enterprise acquisition. Tengel highlighted the company’s focus on "actions we have control over" and confidence in navigating economic uncertainties.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, there was consistent management confidence in strategic initiatives, though analysts appeared more focused on credit quality and economic challenges.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 2025 saw higher credit costs but maintained consistent pre-provision net revenue growth and tangible book value improvements compared to Q4 2024.
  • Deposit cost reductions and intentional CRE concentration reductions remained a focus across both quarters.
  • Analysts in Q1 2025 focused more on the resolution of specific NPLs and credit quality, reflecting ongoing concerns despite progress.
  • Management tone remained steady, with continued emphasis on loan diversification and acquisition integration.

Risks and Concerns

  • Elevated credit costs remain a key risk, tied to large NPLs requiring resolution.
  • Management highlighted potential economic uncertainty from tariffs and federal actions, influencing client expansion decisions.
  • Analysts noted challenges in achieving guidance amid broader economic conditions.

Final Takeaway

Independent Bank Corp. demonstrated resilience in Q1 2025 with strong pre-provision net revenue growth and progress on strategic priorities like loan diversification and deposit growth. Elevated credit costs due to large NPLs remain a challenge, but management expressed confidence in their resolution. The pending Enterprise acquisition is expected to significantly enhance the bank's market presence and capabilities, positioning it for sustained growth in the Northeast.
